metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
no verification commands or testing plan referenced
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

evidence: file_impact is empty
findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for each AC: e.g., `grep -F 'i-00880b2c7f13527c5' infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md` (AC1), `grep -F 'ssm start-session' infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md` (AC2), `diff <(sed -n '74,93p' infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh) ...` cross-check note (AC3), and `git diff --name-only HEAD~1` confirms docs-only (AC4).
evidence: AC1: 'infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md names the prod instance id AND region up front' — no grep command given
AC2: 'Includes the SSM connect command(s)' — no grep command given
AC3: 'The self-update note is accurate' — no command to diff prose against autodeploy.sh:74-93
AC4: 'Docs-only change; no behavior change' — no git diff command given
ABSENCE: zero proving commands in the plan
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is provided for any AC. For a docs change this is still a gap: AC1 and AC2 can be spot-checked with grep anchored to the specific strings, AC3 requires a diff of the written prose against autodeploy.sh:74-93, and AC4 can be confirmed with git diff --name-only. Without stating these commands, an executor (or reviewer) has no in-plan evidence baseline.
scenarios: A reviewer merging the PR has no stated check to confirm the instance ID string actually appears in the file.
AC3 accuracy is hard to audit without a cited command to cross-reference the source lines.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a '## Verification' subsection to the ACs:
```
## Verification
# AC1 – instance + region present
grep -E 'i-00880b2c7f13527c5|us-east-1.*var\.aws_region' infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md
# AC2 – SSM snippet present
grep -F 'ssm start-session' infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md
# AC3 – accuracy spot-check (human: compare note against autodeploy.sh lines 74-93)
cat -n infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh | sed -n '74,93p'
# AC4 – docs-only
git diff --name-only HEAD | grep -v '^infra/runbooks/' && echo 'FAIL: non-doc files changed' || echo 'OK'
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-line maintenance note alongside the instance id pointing to `terraform output instance_id` (in `infra/terraform/`) as the refresh source after an EC2 replacement, mirroring how region is traced to its terraform source of truth.
evidence: Plan states: 'instance i-00880b2c7f13527c5, region us-east-1 (citing var.aws_region / versions.tf as the source of truth)' — region has a living source of truth cited, instance id does not.
infra/terraform/main.tf:104-107: `output "instance_id" { value = aws_instance.gerrit.id }` — a ready-made refresh command exists.

infra/terraform/monitoring.tf:99: '# Resolve the running instance by its Name tag so the status-check alarms bind to the live instance id without hardcoding i-00880b2c7f13527c5' — codebase explicitly documents why NOT to hardcode.
aws_instance.gerrit has no prevent_destroy lifecycle block (main.tf:42-77), so replacement is possible; only the data volume (line 28) and EIP (line 93) are protect_destroy'd.
findings.finding: The plan adds hardcoded instance id `i-00880b2c7f13527c5` with no staleness/refresh guidance. After an EC2 instance replacement (the instance itself has no `prevent_destroy`), the id in the runbook silently stales — recreating the same 'operators guess the id' problem the plan is trying to fix. `main.tf` outputs `instance_id` via `terraform output instance_id` (lines 104-107), and `monitoring.tf` explicitly avoids hardcoding this id for exactly this reason (comment at line 99). The plan traces region to a living terraform source but leaves the instance id with no equivalent 'how to refresh' pointer.
scenarios: EC2 instance is replaced (AMI upgrade, AZ rebalance, or instance type change) — the old id in the runbook now connects to a terminated instance, SSM start-session fails silently or errors, and operators are back to guessing.
A new operator follows the runbook during an incident after a replacement, copies the id verbatim, and loses time on a failed SSM session.
findings.suggested_fix: After listing the instance id, add: `# Refresh after EC2 replacement: cd infra/terraform && terraform output instance_id`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a source-of-truth citation or verification hint for the instance ID (e.g., reference ADR-0026 or include a one-liner such as `aws ec2 describe-instances --filters Name=tag:Name,Values=review-bot --query ...` to confirm the current ID), mirroring the way region is traced to var.aws_region / versions.tf.
evidence: Plan says 'region us-east-1 (citing var.aws_region / versions.tf as the source of truth)' — source is named for region
Plan says 'instance i-00880b2c7f13527c5' with no corresponding citation or verification hint
Plan mentions 'instance id appears in ADR-0026' in the Why section but does not carry that citation into the What / AC
findings.finding: The plan specifies citing var.aws_region / versions.tf as the source of truth for the region, but for the instance ID (i-00880b2c7f13527c5) it names no source of truth, verification command, or cross-reference (e.g., ADR-0026 or `terraform output` / `aws ec2 describe-instances`). If the instance is replaced, the runbook will silently serve a stale ID with no hint to operators that they should verify it.
findings.location: ## What — 'The box' reference section spec / infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md
scenarios: Instance is replaced (spot reclamation, re-provisioning, new terraform apply) — runbook now has wrong ID, operator opens a session to a non-existent or wrong instance
On-call operator has no way to know whether the hardcoded ID is current without a pointer to authoritative state
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'The box' section, after the instance ID line, add a parenthetical: '(verify: `aws ec2 describe-instances --filters Name=tag:Name,Values=review-bot --region us-east-1 --query "Reservations[].Instances[].InstanceId"` or see ADR-0026)'. Also add the instance ID citation to AC bullet 1: 'region traced to var.aws_region / versions.tf; instance ID cross-referenced to ADR-0026 or terraform state output'.

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update (must be hand-deployed)' but infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh lines 74–93 and 92–93 document that it is the INSTALLER (install-autodeploy.sh) and systemd unit files that are excluded from in-run re-materialization — not autodeploy.sh itself. The script body of autodeploy.sh still auto-updates in place via the BOT_PATHS rsync. Encoding the imprecise phrasing into the runbook will mislead operators into thinking the whole autodeploy mechanism needs hand-deploys when only the installer/unit-file layer does.
findings.location: ## What — 'a one-line note that autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update (must be hand-deployed)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'autodeploy.sh is excluded from self-update (must be hand-deployed)' with: 'Note: `autodeploy.sh` (the script body) auto-updates in place on every deploy. The autodeploy *installer* (`install-autodeploy.sh`) and its systemd unit files (`rebar-autodeploy.{service,timer}`) are excluded from in-run re-materialization and must be hand-applied via a direct SSM session when changed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one validation-mechanism AC, e.g. 'An operator (or reviewer who hit the f600 deploy issue) confirmed the SSM connect snippet works end-to-end using only the updated runbook' or a before/after comparison noting what the operator had to look up previously vs. not now.
evidence: 'infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md names the prod instance id AND region up front' — verifies text presence, not operator utility
'Includes the SSM connect command(s)' — structural, not behavioral
No AC reads 'an operator successfully runs the SSM session using only the runbook' or 'reviewed by a team member who faced the f600 deploy issue'
Rubric: 'at least one criterion carries a concrete validation mechanism (before/after workflow comparison, an operational metric target, dogfooding)'
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism is present in the acceptance criteria — all ACs are structural checks on document content ('names the instance id', 'includes SSM command', 'accurate note') rather than an observable workflow improvement or dogfooding step verifying the runbook actually helps the named operator.
scenarios: The runbook is merged with technically correct content but a subtle error (e.g. wrong instance id format, missing --region flag in snippet) that would be caught by a quick dogfooding step but passes all four structural ACs.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: '- [ ] A team member validated the aws ssm start-session snippet runs successfully against i-00880b2c7f13527c5 in us-east-1 using only this runbook as reference (no external lookup needed).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'Why' rationale: the instance id `i-00880b2c7f13527c5` does NOT appear in ADR-0026; update the citation to the actual locations (`infra/runbooks/provision-restore.md`, `infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` header comment, `infra/terraform/monitoring.tf`) so the implementer knows where to ground the fact.
evidence: Search of docs/adr/0026-autodeploy-on-box-timer.md for 'i-00880b2c7f13527c5' returns no matches
Instance id found instead in: infra/runbooks/provision-restore.md:133, infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:10, infra/terraform/monitoring.tf:99
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section claims 'the instance id appears in ADR-0026', but a search of docs/adr/0026-autodeploy-on-box-timer.md finds no occurrence of `i-00880b2c7f13527c5`. The instance id lives in `infra/runbooks/provision-restore.md`, `infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` (header comment), and `infra/terraform/monitoring.tf`. If the implementer follows the plan's citation to ADR-0026 to identify the authoritative source to cite in the new runbook section, they will find no instance id there and may either mis-cite the source or be unable to ground the fact.
findings.location: Why section: 'the instance id appears in ADR-0026'
scenarios: Implementer looks up ADR-0026 to verify/cite the instance id as instructed and cannot find it there, causing confusion about what to cite as the authoritative source in the new runbook section.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the instance id appears in ADR-0026' with 'the instance id appears in infra/runbooks/provision-restore.md:133, infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:10 (header comment), and infra/terraform/monitoring.tf:99'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-line prerequisite note near the `aws ssm start-session` snippet stating that the [Session Manager plugin](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/session-manager-working-with-install-plugin.html) must be installed on the operator's local machine (separate from the AWS CLI).
evidence: Plan states: 'the 'aws ssm start-session'/'send-command' connect snippets'

aws ssm start-session requires the Session Manager plugin (session-manager-plugin binary) on the caller's machine — without it the CLI subcommand exists but exits with 'SessionManagerPlugin is not found' at runtime
The existing runbook (line 298) references send-command but not start-session; send-command does NOT require the local plugin, while start-session does
Evidence class: Partially-verified (plugin requirement is well-documented AWS behaviour, not observed via --help in this environment)
findings.finding: The plan proposes adding `aws ssm start-session` connect snippets but does not state the Session Manager plugin precondition: the operator's local machine must have the AWS Session Manager plugin installed (`session-manager-plugin`) in addition to the AWS CLI, or the `start-session` command will fail with a cryptic error. This is an auth/environment precondition for the named CLI surface.
findings.location: What section / AC: 'Includes the SSM connect command(s)'
scenarios: An operator follows the new runbook snippet, runs `aws ssm start-session --target i-00880b2c7f13527c5 --region us-east-1`, and gets a 'SessionManagerPlugin is not found' error — the runbook gives no hint of why or how to fix it, defeating the purpose of the section.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single prerequisite line before the start-session snippet, e.g.: `> **Prereq:** the [Session Manager plugin](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/session-manager-working-with-install-plugin.html) must be installed locally (`session-manager-plugin`) — `send-command` does not need it.`
